data.title: Cathay General Bancorp adopts $150M buyback program
Stock index futures gain ahead of more labor reports
data.description:
Stock index futures advanced on Thursday, as traders braced for another batch of labor data.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.4%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.3%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.37%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.90%.

Wall Street on Wednesday closed little changed, while bonds rallied after private hiring in May reached its lowest level in over two years.

"Markets put in a strong performance yesterday, after a weak batch of US data led to a massive rally for U.S.Treasuries, which in turn supported risk assets," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

Of course, even with the rally, the data still raised fears that the U.S. economy lost steam into May. That started off with the ADP’s report of private payrolls, which was the softest in over two years, Allen added. 

In the day ahead, the initial jobless claims and productivity and costs reports will land before the opening bell. The former is expected to fall to 236K.

"U.S. first quarter productivity data is not market moving. Actual productivity matters enormously to long-term trend growth. Technology should boost productivity but also increases fear of the future," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

The main event in terms of labor data—the monthly employment reading—will land on Friday.

data.title: Verint Systems shares surge as earnings beat estimates on AI demand
data.description:
Shares of Verint Systems (NASDAQ: NASDAQ:VRNT) jumped 20% in premarket trading Thursday after the company beat quarterly earnings estimates, fueled by strong demand and a growing pipeline for its AI-powered solutions.

Q1 ARR growth accelerated to 6% year-over-year, reflecting continued AI momentum

 
 

Dan Bodner, Chairman and CEO, emphasized Verint's differentiation through its ability to "transform the latest AI technology into strong, tangible AI business outcomes delivering customer value better than any other CX vendor" and highlighted the company's hybrid cloud model for rapid AI deployment.

"We kicked off the year strong, expect our AI momentum to continue and are targeting exiting the year with 8% ARR growth and double-digit free cash flow growth," Bodner stated.

The company's first quarter revenue was reported at $208 million, with non-GAAP diluted EPS of $0.29.

CFO Grant Highlander indicated the revenue "overachievement was primarily due to the timing of two large, unbundled SaaS deals and therefore, does not impact our revenue and diluted EPS outlook for the year."

The company maintained its annual guidance.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Ratings, the company has a Strong Sell rating with a score of 1.39 out of 5.

data.title: EU sees progress in trade talks with U.S. despite higher metals levies
data.description:
Despite Trump’s recent decision to double tariffs on steel and aluminum imports, EU's trade negotiator Maros Sefcovic said that their trade talks are making progress and moving in the “right direction” after meeting with U.S. trade representative Jamieson Greer in Paris on the sidelines of an OECD conference.
 
"We both concluded that we are advancing in the right direction, at pace," Sefcovic told reporters. 
"What makes me optimistic is I see the progress ... the discussions are now very concrete," Sefcovic said. 
 
When asked about Trump’s doubling of the metals tariffs, Sefcovic said it wasn’t a helpful step and urged that the two nations work together to resolve overcapacity problems, according to a Bloomberg report.
 
“We told him that we strongly regret this latest US decision and explained to him it clearly doesn’t help the ongoing negotiations especially as we are making progress,” he said. “So my plea was, ‘let’s not target each other but work together.’”
 
Greer said the talks were advancing quickly and demonstrated "a willingness by the EU to work with us to find a concrete way forward to achieve reciprocal trade."
 
The EU has offered a “zero for zero” deal to eliminate tariffs on industrial goods, including autos, but the U.S. has so far rejected this proposal. The European Commission promised to speed up the trade talks after Trump pointed the 27-nation organization of slow-walking negotiations.
 

Here is a list of ETFs tracking the European markets: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK).

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: MDB, TOPS, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher in Thursday’s premarket session as investors awaited the latest U.S. jobless claims data, with attention also turning to Friday’s closely watched nonfarm payrolls report.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • MongoDB (NASDAQ:MDB) +15% – Shares rallied after the database company posted better-than-expected Q1 results, raised its full-year guidance, and expanded its share buyback program. For Q2, MongoDB expects revenue of $548M–$553M and adjusted EPS of $0.62–$0.66, above analyst estimates of $549.28M and $0.58, respectively. Full-year revenue is now forecast at $2.25B–$2.29B, up from $2.24B–$2.28B, while adjusted EPS is projected at $2.94–$3.12, significantly above the previous $2.44–$2.62 range and the $2.65 consensus.
  • Verint Systems (NASDAQ:VRNT) +21% – Shares surged after the company delivered better-than-expected Q1 results, highlighted by a 24% Y/Y increase in AI annual recurring revenue (AI ARR), which includes its AI-powered software offerings. Verint expects AI ARR to grow more than 20% for the full year, an acceleration from last year. The company is targeting 8% ARR growth overall, and with expanding cash contribution margins, it anticipates a 12% increase in free cash flow for the year.
  • Five Below (NASDAQ:FIVE) +5% – Shares gained after the specialty discount retailer reported a top- and bottom-line beat for Q1, with comparable sales rising 7.1%, ahead of the 6.4% consensus. The company opened 55 new stores during the quarter, bringing the total to 1,826 locations across 44 states, a 13.8% increase Y/Y. For Q2, Five Below guided net sales between $975M and $995M, ahead of the $958.3M consensus. Adjusted EPS is expected in the range of $0.50 to $0.62 (midpoint $0.56 vs. estimate $0.58), with guidance factoring in the impact of tariffs. The company expects 7% to 9% growth in comparable sales and plans to open approximately 30 net new stores. Separately, Five Below announced that CFO Kristy Chipman will step down for personal reasons. Current COO Ken Bull, who previously served as CFO for over a decade, will assume the finance chief duties on an interim basis.

Biggest stock losers

  • TOP Ships (NYSE:TOPS) -12% – Shares fell after a 44% rally in the previous session, as the company announced plans to spin off two Suezmax tanker vessels into a new publicly traded entity, Rubico. Rubico, currently a subsidiary of TOP Ships, will become an independent Nasdaq-listed company, holding the M/T Eco Malibu and M/T Eco West Coast—modern, high-spec, fuel-efficient tankers. As part of the spin-off, TOP Ships will distribute 100% of Rubico’s common shares to shareholders and warrant holders of record as of June 16, 2025. Rubico also expects to raise $1.5M via a private placement at $20 per share.

data.title: Alvotech and Dr. Reddy’s partner to co-develop biosimilar candidate for Keytruda (pembrolizumab)
data.description:
Alvotech (NASDAQ:ALVO) and Dr. Reddy’s Laboratories (NYSE:RDY) have entered into a collaboration and license agreement to co-develop, manufacture, and commercialize a biosimilar candidate to Keytruda (pembrolizumab) for global markets.

Keytruda (pembrolizumab) is indicated for the treatment of numerous cancer types. In 2024, worldwide sales of Keytruda were $29.5 billion.

The collaboration combines Dr. Reddy’s (NYSE:RDY) and Alvotech’s (NASDAQ:ALVO) proven capabilities in biosimilars, thereby, accelerating the development process and extending the global reach for this biosimilar candidate.

Under the terms of the agreement, the parties will be jointly responsible for developing and manufacturing the biosimilar candidate and sharing costs and responsibilities. Subject to certain exceptions, each party will have the right to commercialize the product globally.

Source: Press Release

data.description:

  • Revenue of $1.35B (-0.7% Y/Y).

data.title: Hello Group reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Hello Group press release (NASDAQ:MOMO): Q1 Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.32.
  • Revenue of $347.4M (-2.0% Y/Y) beats by $11.93M.
  • Diluted net income per American Depositary Share ("ADS") was RMB2.07 (US$0.29) in the first quarter of 2025, compared to RMB0.03 in the same period of 2024.
  • Monthly Active Users ("MAU") on Tantan app were 10.7 million in March 2025, compared to 13.7 million in March 2024.
  • For the Momo app total paying users was 4.2 million for the first quarter of 2025, compared to 7.1 million for the same period last year. Tantan had 0.8 million paying users for the first quarter of 2025 compared to 1.1 million from the year ago period.
  • For the second quarter of 2025, the Company expects total net revenues to be between RMB2.57 billion to RMB2.67 billion, representing a decrease of 4.5% to 0.8% year over year. This forecast reflects the Company's current and preliminary views on the market and operational conditions, which are subject to change.

data.title: Auto industry braces for supply chain disruptions from China's rare earth curbs
data.description:
The automotive industry is bracing for supply chain disruptions due to China's export restrictions on rare earth elements and magnets, which were imposed in retaliation to President Donald Trump's tariffs.

China dominates the critical mineral supply chain, producing about 90% of the world's rare earths that are widely used in the auto, energy, semiconductor, and defense industries.

"The European automotive supply industry is already experiencing significant disruption," auto supplier association CLEPA has warned, adding that the impacted auto parts are critical to combustion engine and electric vehicles. 

"These restrictions have led to the shutdown of several production lines and plants across Europe, with further impacts expected in the coming weeks as inventories deplete," the group noted.

While the European Union is reportedly in talks with China to address the issue, the bloc is looking to diversify supply chains. The EU on Wednesday identified 13 critical raw material projects overseas to ensure sustainable supply, including in the UK, Ukraine, Greenland, Canada, and South Africa.

Industry impact

Some of BMW's (OTCPK:BAMXF) supplier network has been disrupted by China's export controls, but the automaker's own plants are running as normal, Reuters reported.

Joerg Burzer, production chief at Mercedes-Benz (OTCPK:MBGAF), said the company is not currently impacted, but it is discussing with suppliers possible "physical buffers" for its supply chain.

A Volkswagen (OTCPK:VWAGY) spokesperson told CNBC that while the automaker's supply chain is stable, its suppliers have indicated that only a limited number of rare earth export licenses have been granted by Chinese authorities.

In a conference on Wednesday, Ford (NYSE:F) CFO Sherry House said there have been some delays in procuring rare earths, but the company is managing the issue for now. "It just puts stress on a system that's highly organized, with parts being ordered many weeks in advance."

Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY) CEO Ivan Espinosa told CNBC that the automaker is looking at ways to minimize the impact of the export controls. Suzuki (OTCPK:SZKMY) has reportedly halted production of its Swift car temporarily.

data.title: European indexes flat as investors await ECB interest rate decision
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.01% to 8,802, staying flat for the fourth straight day as mixed corporate news weigh investor sentiment.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.12% to 24,315, surpassing 24,300 to hit a new milestone and extend its winning streak to three consecutive days. The HCOB Germany Construction PMI slipped to 44.4 in May. Factory orders in Germany rose 0.6% M/M in April.

France (CAC:IND) +0.15% to 7,816, The HCOB Construction PMI in France fell to 43.1 in May.

The HCOB Eurozone Construction PMI fell to 45.6 in May.

The HCOB Italy Construction PMI edged up to 50.5 in May.

Finland’s trade deficit narrowed to €0.10B in April. Slovenia posted a trade deficit of €446.5M in April.

Sweden’s current account surplus widened sharply to SEK 119.3B in Q1.

The Swiss unemployment rate remained stable at a non-seasonally adjusted 2.8% in May.

Wholesale prices in Austria fell by 0.5% Y/Y in May.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) gained 0.21% to 552.2, as investors refrained from making big bets ahead of the European Central Bank’s monetary policy decision later in the day. European natural gas futures climbed above €36 per megawatt-hour as supply from Norway, the region’s top gas exporter, dropped due to planned maintenance at the Kollsnes processing plant.

Coming up in the session: ECB interest rate decision is lined up for the day, where it expected to cut interest rates by another 25 basis points at its June meeting as it seeks to support growth amid slowing inflation and ongoing global trade uncertainties.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 1 basis point to 4.38%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 1 basis point to 2.53%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 4.62%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)
